title: Integrate Camera Recording Directly into Live Short Upload Flow

requirements.text: On the UploadVideoPage, add a seamless 'Record & Upload Short' flow: when the user opens the CameraRecordingModal and completes a recording, automatically set the 'Mark as Short' toggle to enabled, populate the upload form with the recorded Blob as the selected file, and allow the user to immediately submit the upload — all within a single continuous UI flow without leaving the page.

quotes: Camera upload live short upload ek kare

acceptanceCriteria: The UploadVideoPage has a clearly labeled 'Record & Upload Short' button that opens the CameraRecordingModal.
After the user stops recording and confirms the clip in the modal, the recorded Blob is set as the selected video file in the upload form.
The 'Mark as Short' toggle is automatically enabled when a video is recorded via the camera recording flow.
The duration of the recorded clip is validated client-side; if it exceeds 60 seconds, a clear warning is shown and the Short toggle is unchecked.
The title and description fields are pre-populated or left editable so the user can complete and submit without re-selecting a file.
The entire flow — record, preview, confirm, fill form details, upload — is achievable without navigating away from the UploadVideoPage.
If the user cancels the modal, the upload form state is unchanged.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Reuse the existing CameraRecordingModal component and useCameraRecorder hook; do not replace them.
Reuse the existing useUploadVideo hook for the actual upload call.
nonGoals: Adding a new separate page or route for camera-only uploads.
Changing the backend video data model or upload API.
Supporting camera upload from the Reels page in this request.
